_about: Worked example reproducing Aswath Damodaran's NVIDIA valuation (Musings on Markets, 23 Jun 2023). All $ are in MILLIONS USD; rates are decimals. Running dcf_valuation.py on this file yields ~$236/share vs the $409 price on 2023-06-10 -> overvalued, reproducing his ~$240 conclusion.
_driver1_growth: Base = TTM revenue through Q1 FY24 ($25.88B). Growth declines from ~50% to the riskfree rate over 10 yrs, hitting Damodaran's $267B in year 10 (AI + Auto + Gaming bottoms-up).
_driver2_margin: R&D-adjusted pre-tax operating margin recovers from a 2022 setback (~32%) to a 40% target by 2027 (year 4), held in perpetuity.
_driver3_reinvestment: Sales-to-capital rises to the semiconductor industry median of 1.15 (from NVDA's historic 0.65). Reinvestment = change-in-revenue / 1.15.
_driver4_risk: Cost of capital starts at the US semiconductor industry average 12.21% (initial growth period, yrs 1-5) and glides to the market-average mature 8.85% by year 10.
_tax_note: NVDA's structural effective tax rate is low (~13-15%: IP location, stock-comp deductions, FDII). Damodaran's headline value uses a low rate. Ramping to a 25% marginal rate (set effective_tax_rate=0.15, tax_rate=0.25, tax_convergence_year=10) lowers value to ~$200 -- still far below $409, so the OVERVALUED conclusion is robust.
_terminal: Stable growth = riskfree rate (3.85%). Terminal ROC of 12% reflects a durable CUDA-driven moat earning a modest excess return in perpetuity (terminal reinvestment rate = g / ROC).
_bridge: Operating assets -> equity: + cash & marketable securities, - total debt; ~2,470M shares (pre-2024 10:1 split).
